etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 阿(阿)
etymology.interpretation: The character 啊 (a) combines 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") indicating something spoken or uttered, with 阿(阿) providing the sound. The component 口 illustrates that this term is used for vocal expression, specifically as a particle or exclamation.

examples.chinese: 今天天气真好啊！(今天天氣真好啊！)
examples.english: The weather is really nice today!

examples.chinese: 快来看啊，他在这儿呢！(快來看啊，他在這兒呢！)
examples.pinyin: Kuài lái kàn a, tā zài zhè'er ne!

examples.chinese: 你听我说啊，这事情很重要！(你聽我說啊，這事情很重要！)
examples.pinyin: Nǐ tīng wǒ shuō a, zhè shìqíng hěn zhòngyào!
examples.english: Listen to me, this is very important!
